category_definition: The scope of what is included and excluded from the register
indicator_text: The following types of lobbyists are included in the register: professional lobbyists/consultants, in-house company lobbyists, in-house organisation lobbyists, professional consultancies, law firms, think tanks, research institutions, public entities, government agencies/employees

category_definition: The scope of what is included and excluded from the register
indicator_text: There is no (or low) financial or time threshold to qualify/exempt lobbyists from registration
measurement_guidance: Paper notes thresholds are context-dependent; what counts as "low" varies by country/state.

category_definition: The scope of what is included and excluded from the register
indicator_text: The following are included as targets of lobbying: legislative branches, executive branch officials, Ministers, Deputy Ministers, members of parliament, Director-Generals and senior officials, staff, administrative branch/bureaucracy
measurement_guidance: Westminster-centric role names; US-state application must translate to governor, lt. governor, legislators, agency heads, staff, etc.

category_definition: The scope of what is included and excluded from the register
indicator_text: A wide breadth of activities are included, eg, oral, written, electronic, virtual communications; organising meetings for others; events; phone calls and emails

category_definition: The frequency of lobbying disclosures
indicator_text: Changes (eg, registering/deregistering lobbyists, new clients) are updated close to real time (eg, daily)
measurement_guidance: Paper cites International Standards for Lobbying Transparency: minimum quarterly, ideally close to real-time.

category_definition: The frequency of lobbying disclosures
indicator_text: Lobbying activities are disclosed close to real time (eg, daily)

category_definition: The frequency of lobbying disclosures
indicator_text: Ministerial diaries are disclosed monthly (or more frequently)
measurement_guidance: "Ministerial diary" is a UK/Australia concept; US-state analog is executive calendar/meeting log disclosure, which is rare at state level.

category_definition: How easy it is to find and use information in the register
indicator_text: Lobbyist register is online

category_definition: How easy it is to find and use information in the register
indicator_text: Diaries available online (eg, lobbyists, ministers, ministerial staff)
measurement_guidance: See 2.3 note on ministerial diary terminology.

category_definition: How easy it is to find and use information in the register
indicator_text: Available without registration, free to access, open license (eg, no limits to reuse), non-proprietary format (eg, CSV, not Excel), machine readable
measurement_guidance: Compound indicator: five sub-conditions (no registration, free, open license, non-proprietary format, machine readable). Scoring may require decomposition.

category_definition: How easy it is to find and use information in the register
indicator_text: Downloadable (eg, as files, database)

category_definition: How easy it is to find and use information in the register
indicator_text: Searchable, simultaneous sorting with multiple criteria

category_definition: How easy it is to find and use information in the register
indicator_text: Unique identifiers (eg, for lobbyists, individuals, organisations)

category_definition: How easy it is to find and use information in the register
indicator_text: Linked or interconnected data (to other datasets, eg, campaign financing)

category_definition: How easy it is to find and use information in the register
indicator_text: Historical data in lobbyist register is archived and published; downloadable

category_definition: How easy it is to find and use information in the register
indicator_text: Changes or updates documented with a flagging system

category_definition: Descriptions and identifying elements of the individuals and organisations involved in lobbying
indicator_text: Full names of lobbyists/organisations, (not abbreviations or ambiguous names)

category_definition: Descriptions and identifying elements of the individuals and organisations involved in lobbying
indicator_text: Contact details provided (eg, Address, telephone and/or website)

category_definition: Descriptions and identifying elements of the individuals and organisations involved in lobbying
indicator_text: Legal form (eg, public, private, not-for-profit, NGO, government)

category_definition: Descriptions and identifying elements of the individuals and organisations involved in lobbying
indicator_text: Company registration number
measurement_guidance: US analog: state Secretary-of-State business entity ID or federal EIN.

category_definition: Descriptions and identifying elements of the individuals and organisations involved in lobbying
indicator_text: Sector (eg, transport, energy), sub sector

category_definition: Descriptions and identifying elements of the individuals and organisations involved in lobbying
indicator_text: Type of lobbyist contract (eg, salaried staff, contracted)

category_definition: The movement between public and private sector employment
indicator_text: List of all prior public offices that lobbyists have held, dates when left office

category_definition: The movement between public and private sector employment
indicator_text: Database of public officials who are subject to a ban on lobbying (eg, cooling off period)
measurement_guidance: Paper notes only 4 of 15 source frameworks covered revolving door; cooling-off period is a common operationalization.

category_definition: The connections between the different actors involved in or benefiting from lobbying
indicator_text: Client list (for all consultant lobbyists and firms)

category_definition: The connections between the different actors involved in or benefiting from lobbying
indicator_text: Names of all sponsors or members (for associations and representative groups)

category_definition: The connections between the different actors involved in or benefiting from lobbying
indicator_text: List of board seats held (eg, in associations, companies)

category_definition: The connections between the different actors involved in or benefiting from lobbying
indicator_text: Direct business associations with public officials, candidates or members of their households

category_definition: The flow of money spent and earned through lobbying activities
indicator_text: Total lobbying income (for consultant lobbyists/lobby firms)
measurement_guidance: Applies to consultant lobbyists & lobby firms (paper subheading).

category_definition: The flow of money spent and earned through lobbying activities
indicator_text: Lobbying income per client
measurement_guidance: Applies to consultant lobbyists & lobby firms.

category_definition: The flow of money spent and earned through lobbying activities
indicator_text: Income sources (eg, including government agencies, grant-making foundations, companies) and amount received
measurement_guidance: Applies to consultant lobbyists & lobby firms.

category_definition: The flow of money spent and earned through lobbying activities
indicator_text: Number of lobbyists employed/contracted (total and full-time equivalent)

category_definition: The flow of money spent and earned through lobbying activities
indicator_text: Amount of time spent on lobbying

category_definition: The flow of money spent and earned through lobbying activities
indicator_text: Total lobbying expenditure (both in-house and consulting)

category_definition: The flow of money spent and earned through lobbying activities
indicator_text: Compensated/uncompensated lobbying activities

category_definition: The flow of money spent and earned through lobbying activities
indicator_text: Expenditure per issue

category_definition: The flow of money spent and earned through lobbying activities
indicator_text: Expenditure on membership/sponsorship of organisations that lobby (eg, trade associations)

category_definition: The flow of money spent and earned through lobbying activities
indicator_text: Expenditures benefitting public officials or employees including financial/non-financial gifts and support, employer/principal on whose behalf expenses were made

category_definition: The flow of money spent and earned through lobbying activities
indicator_text: Campaign/political contributions, including in-kind

category_definition: The activities of lobbyists
indicator_text: Organisation/interest(s) represented (beneficiary)

category_definition: The activities of lobbyists
indicator_text: Names of persons contacted and their position/role

category_definition: The activities of lobbyists
indicator_text: Institution/department contacted

category_definition: The activities of lobbyists
indicator_text: If a meeting, names of all attendees

category_definition: The activities of lobbyists
indicator_text: Date

category_definition: The activities of lobbyists
indicator_text: Form (eg, in person meeting, video conference, phone call)

category_definition: The activities of lobbyists
indicator_text: Location

category_definition: The activities of lobbyists
indicator_text: Any materials that were shared, excluding commercially sensitive materials (before, during and after the meeting)

category_definition: The activities of lobbyists
indicator_text: Topics/issues discussed

category_definition: The activities of lobbyists
indicator_text: Outcomes sought (eg, legislation/policies supported/opposed)

category_definition: The activities of lobbyists
indicator_text: Targeted areas of public policy or legislation, including a list of official legislative references/bill numbers/measures etc
